history.content:
<thought>
The goal is visible to the upper right, but the cell directly in front of me contains lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. The cell to my right is open floor, so I should turn right to face that safe path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible to my upper-left, but I am currently facing upward and the cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. Turning left would orient me toward the open floor cell on my left, which is the safe direction that also moves me toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead to the right, but the cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. The open safe path is to my right, so I should rotate clockwise first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead and to my left relative to my current view. The cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. The cells to the left side form a safe corridor toward the goal, while the right side is blocked by a wall. I should turn left to face the safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible to the upper right, but the cell directly in front of me contains lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. The open floor is to my right, so I should turn right to face a safe path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible to my upper-left, but I am currently facing upward and the cell directly in front of me contains lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. The wall to my right blocks that side. Turning left is the best next step to reorient toward the safe open cells on my left and eventually approach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead to the right, but the cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. The open path is to my right, so I should rotate clockwise first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible to my upper-left, but I am facing upward and the cell directly in front of me is floor, while walls block the right side. To move toward the goal safely, I should first advance into the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible to my left in the row behind the wall boundary, while the cell directly in front of me is open floor. Turning toward the goal is not immediately helpful because moving left or right from my current orientation is blocked or unclear, and the safest progress is to step forward into the open cell to explore a path around the wall.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is immediately to my left in the visible corridor, while the cell directly in front of me is blocked by a wall. Turning left will orient me toward the goal without stepping into danger.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom-center facing upward. The cell directly in front is safe floor, while the goal is visible ahead and to the left. Turning left would face lava, and turning right would face a wall. The best safe progress is to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
